what_it_does: A Round-2 completeness critique that audits whether post-fold edits to a concurrent-cognition design triad fully propagated two headline decisions (use run:// not swarm://; size concurrency by max_num_seqs=16 not 32) into the document bodies, lists remaining contradictions, missing HOW specifications, drift-home gaps, and verification ambiguities, and maps each gap to the build gate it blocks.
observations: Tier 0 flags live contradictions that survived the fold: swarm:// is still treated as resolvable in C4.2, Guide G0 step 3, Guide G5, and Synthesis, while the headers and C1.3 say run://.
Three live assertions of 32 concurrency remain in Synthesis and Guide bodies, contradicting the banner and adjacent lines that state max_num_seqs=16.
The G0 spike instruction tells a build agent to resolve a swarm:// address, which the file argues is unbuildable because swarm is not a registered address scheme.

Tier 1 notes fold demands not reflected in the Criteria: self-description/drift homes are not named for every net-new registry, C4.2 is still framed as reuse rather than net-new, cognition.* is still called an emit-contract, and C1.3 tells builders to bump Edge.schema_ver which the file says does not exist.

Tier 2 calls out under-specified net-new subsystems: G5's three activation triggers lack host mechanism and TurnContext-without-utterance details, THOUGHT_SHAPES has no record schema, typed-lane/channel has no seam, and the jury verdict/draw-addressing evaluator is mostly unspecified.

Tier 3 identifies verification-path gaps: C0.2 replay determinism is ambiguous against temp>0 varied draws, the swarm's routing through the scheduler vs direct run_role is undecided, and no adversarial test proves background/sense cannot starve the reserved floor.
Tier 4 positively records that all six binding decisions from DECISIONS.md are reflected somewhere in the Criteria.
Tier 5 lists smaller carried items: stale suite.py line references, unstated G6 transitive dependency on the chat_parts refactor, and unguarded VRAM co-residence for the swarm KV pool.
The verdict says the architecture is sound, G0 is not clean-buildable as written but cheap to fix, and fan-out past G0 is blocked on net-new HOW and drift homes.
standouts: The file's core claim is that the R1 fold half-landed: headers were corrected but the bodies a build agent reads still contain contradictory instructions.
It argues the very first buildable artefact, G0, contains an unbuildable instruction because of a surviving swarm:// resolution.
Net-new subsystems such as activation triggers, thought-shape schema, jury aggregation, and typed-lane seam are named but lack enough specification to verify.
It includes a positive completeness table confirming all six binding decisions appear in the Criteria, even while noting their HOW gaps.
conventions: Follows a severity-tiered structure (T0–T5) with file:line-anchored findings and a non-binary, gate-blocking verdict.
Uses R1-FOLD.md, DECISIONS.md, and prior R1 review files as reference points without reproducing them.
Breaks pure read-only audit convention by prescribing exact text edits and reclassifications.
Uses inline code, markdown tables, and parenthetical line references throughout.
concerns: The file itself contains evaluative ranking language such as 'single most important R2 finding' and 'central failure,' which is inconsistent with a neutral audit posture.
It mixes observation and prescription, so a reader must separate findings from recommended fixes.
Line-number anchors are precise but will drift as the audited documents are edited.
It asserts build-agent behavior ('a build agent reads bodies, not headers') without evidence drawn from this file alone.
notes: A post-fold audit arguing the design's headers are reconciled but the bodies still contain build-blocking contradictions and under-specified net-new subsystems, with a gate-by-gate fix map.
questions: What are the exact current contents of the triad documents being audited, since only excerpts are quoted?
Where are the authoritative definitions of gates G0–G8 and how are they versioned?
What process verifies that a fold has propagated into every document body before a build starts?
What do drift_acceptance.py and the MAP.md registry block look like structurally?
How does a run:// address relate to the existing _chat_context/_resolve_context_at path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a read-only Round-2 pass asking what is still missing, unverified, contradictory, or unscoped after the R1 fold; the actual content does that and also prescribes specific text edits and classifies each gap by the build gate it blocks, making it more prescriptive than purely read-only.
apparent_themes: Propagation of design decisions from headers into implementation-facing bodies
intention_signals: It tries to prevent a build agent from implementing stale or contradictory instructions by forcing a second propagation pass and by demanding concrete HOW and drift homes for every net-new subsystem before parallel work expands.
novelty: It treats documentation-body consistency as a first-class build artefact and maps textual contradictions directly to gate-blocking failures rather than treating them as cosmetic.
contribution: It supplies a structured, gate-indexed inventory of post-fold gaps that a fused build-planning system can use as a reconciliation checklist before coding starts.
summary_for_embedding: R2-completeness.md is a post-fold audit of a concurrent-cognition design triad that catalogs surviving contradictions, missing drift homes, under-specified net-new subsystems, and verification ambiguities, then maps each to the build gate it blocks.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to verify that the R1 fold's headline decisions propagated into the document bodies and to identify what must still be specified before the G0 spike and subsequent fan-out can be built to a verifiable bar.
